reviews.review_msg: Class was hard, but learned a lot about micro economics in the SFU MBA course. You can tell the prof is super engaged and passionate about the course being taught. Tied a lot of content and group work into real world examples. Exams were a bit difficult and had a lot of pre-reading material before it even started. Would still take it again though.

This course was already regarded as the most difficult one among all BBA courses. Considering that she was the one teaching it, one can imagine how poor the pass rate and learning experience would be for this course. The midterm average was 47%, and the TA (Yuting)'s attitude was bad; she felt she was the prof...arbitrarily set the scores.

Her lectures were interesting, especially when we linked concepts to real life! She gives weekly in-class assignments (we correct them in-class), and most grades come from 1 midterm and final. Do the practice problems and review econ theories she teaches, as they relate to test material. Otherwise tests are based on pre-recorded videos and lecture.

Her lectures are extremely boring and very disengaging. The exam is extremely hard, marked by the TA. The MT average was just 47%, and even when asking the TA about deduction marks, she just repeated my answer and not a direct answer to what I asked and finally said, "Sorry, I don't know..."

She is a poor choice to choose for BUS207. The midterm average was only 46%; the other instructors' sections are 65% and 72%. You could see how people hate this difficult class and her section! Avoid please...

Content on exams is heavily based on lectures. Lectures can be interesting at times but for the most part are mild. Exams are straightforward and questions are fair and consist of short answer and calculation questions.

She is very accessible and approachable so if you do not understand a concept, just ask her to explain in during break or after class. Importantly, the tests are directly related to the practice problems she provides, so if you can do those, you are good to go. I did not understand all the concepts at first go, it was crucial that I asked her.

Bus207 is a tough course generally but Professor Peng delivers the contents very effectively. She tests on things she actually teaches and you will certainly do well if you pay attention to her lectures. Textbook is not a must, her slides are helpful enough and cover all the content.

She has one of the most innovative teaching styles out there. She breaks down the material into 5-20 minute sections so it is easier to focus on the concept being taught. The assignments and practice questions had thorough solutions which really help one learn the content.
Great Prof, truly passionate about what she teaches! Thank you Peng
